Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Take a [G] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

Name: Celebi ex
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

Name: Snivy
Flavor-Text: Being exposed to sunlight makes its movements swifter. It uses vines more adeptly than its hands.

Name: Servine
Flavor-Text: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

Name: Serperior
Ability-Effect: Each [G] Energy attached to your [G] Pokémon provides 2 [G] Energy. This effect doesn't stack.
Flavor-Text: It only gives its all against strong opponents who are not fazed by the glare from Serperior’s noble eyes.

Name: Morelull
Flavor-Text: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

Name: Shiinotic
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

Name: Dhelmise
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra [G] Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

Name: Ponyta
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

Name: Larvesta
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was called the Larva That Stole the Sun. The fire Larvesta spouts from its horns can cut right through a sheet of iron.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its burning body causes it to be unpopular in hot parts of the world, but in cold ones, Volcarona is revered as an embodiment of the sun.

Name: Salandit
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

Name: Salazzle
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

Name: Gyarados ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

Name: Vaporeon
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a [W] Energy from 1 of your Benched [W] Pokémon to your Active [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

Name: Finneon
Flavor-Text: The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.

Name: Lumineon
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With its shining light, it lures its prey close. However, the light also happens to attract ferocious fish Pokémon—its natural predators.

Name: Chewtle
Flavor-Text: Its large front tooth is still growing in. When the tooth itches, this Pokémon will bite another Chewtle’s horn, and the two Pokémon will tussle.

Name: Drednaw
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its massive, jagged teeth can crush a boulder in a single bite. This Pokémon has an extremely vicious disposition.

Name: Cramorant
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it’s battling midfight.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each of your Benched [L] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Raichu
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

Name: Joltik
Flavor-Text: Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It’s soaking up static electricity because it can’t produce a charge on its own.

Name: Galvantula
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It’s small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people’s homes and charge itself.

Name: Mew
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

Name: Mew ex
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

Name: Sigilyph
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians’ emissaries.

Name: Elgyem
Flavor-Text: If this Pokémon stands near a TV, strange scenery will appear on the screen. That scenery is said to be from its home.

Name: Beheeyem
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Whenever a Beheeyem visits a farm, a Dubwool mysteriously disappears.

Name: Flabébé
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can draw forth the power hidden within blooming wild flowers. It is particularly fond of red flowers.

Name: Floette
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon draws forth what power is left in withered flowers to make them healthy again. It holds a red flower.

Name: Florges
Effects: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon creates an impressive flower garden in its territory. It draws forth the power of the red flowers around its neck.

Name: Swirlix
Flavor-Text: It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn’t get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.

Name: Slurpuff
Flavor-Text: By taking in a person’s scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It’s hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

Name: Primeape
Flavor-Text: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

Name: Geodude
Flavor-Text: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

Name: Graveler
Flavor-Text: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

Name: Golem
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

Name: Aerodactyl ex
Ability-Effect: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Active Pokémon.

Name: Marshadow
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It slips into the shadows of others and mimics their powers and movements. As it improves, it becomes stronger than those it’s imitating.

Name: Stonjourner
Flavor-Text: The elemental composition of the rocks that form its body were found to match the bedrock of a land far away from this Pokémon’s habitat.

Name: Koffing
Effects: Put 1 random Koffing from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

Name: Weezing
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.
Flavor-Text: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

Name: Purrloin
Flavor-Text: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

Name: Liepard
Flavor-Text: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

Name: Venipede
Flavor-Text: Venipede and Sizzlipede are similar species, but when the two meet, a huge fight ensues.

Name: Whirlipede
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon spins itself rapidly and charges into its opponents. Its top speed is just over 60 mph.

Name: Scolipede
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Scolipede latches on to its prey with the claws on its neck before slamming them into the ground and jabbing them with its claws’ toxic spikes.

Name: Druddigon
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Druddigon lives in caves, but it never skips sunbathing—it won’t be able to move if its body gets too cold.

Name: Pidgey
Flavor-Text: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

Name: Pidgeotto
Flavor-Text: The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

Name: Pidgeot ex
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

Name: Tauros
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 80  more damage.
Flavor-Text: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.
Flavor-Text: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them. This way they won’t attack it.

Name: Old Amber
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Pokemon Flute
Description: Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent's discard pile onto their Bench.

Name: Mythical Slab
Description: Look at the top card of your deck. If that card is a [P] Pokémon, put it into your hand. If it is not a [P] Pokémon, put it on the bottom of your deck.

Name: Budding Expeditioner
Description: Put your Mew ex in the Active Spot into your hand.

Name: Blue
Description: During your opponent's next turn, all of your Pokémon take −10 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Leaf
Description: During this turn, the Retreat Cost of your Active Pokémon is 2 less.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

Ability-Effect: Each [G] Energy attached to your [G] Pokémon provides 2 [G] Energy. This effect doesn't stack.
Flavor-Text: It only gives its all against strong opponents who are not fazed by the glare from Serperior’s noble eyes.

Flavor-Text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

Effects: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
